pg157-p2-s1:
Two days later, a future fellow of Alcoholics Anonymous stared glassily at the strangers beside his bed. "Who are you
fellows, and why this private room?
pg157-p2-s2: I was always in a ward before."
pg157-p2:
Two days later, a future fellow of Alcoholics Anonymous stared glassily at the strangers beside his bed. "Who are you
fellows, and why this private room? I was always in a ward before."
pg157-p3-s1: Said one of the visitors, "We're giving you a treatment for alcoholism."
pg157-p3: Said one of the visitors, "We're giving you a treatment for alcoholism."
pg157-p4-s1: Hopelessness was written large on the man's face as he replied, "Oh, but that's no use.
pg157-p4-s4: The last three times, I got drunk on the way home from here.
pg157-p4-s5: I'm afraid to go out the door.
pg157-p4:
Hopelessness was written large on the man's face as he replied, "Oh, but that's no use. Nothing would fix me. I'm a
goner. The last three times, I got drunk on the way home from here. I'm afraid to go out the door. I can't understand
it."
pg157-p5-s1: For an hour, the two friends told him about their drinking experiences.
pg157-p5-s2: Over and over, he would say: "That's me.
pg157-p5:
For an hour, the two friends told him about their drinking experiences. Over and over, he would say: "That's me. That's
me. I drink like that."
pg157-p6-s1:
The man in the bed was told of the acute poisoning from which he suffered, how it deteriorates the body of an alcoholic
and warps his mind.
pg157-p6-s2: There was much talk about the mental state preceding the first drink.
pg157-p6:
The man in the bed was told of the acute poisoning from which he suffered, how it deteriorates the body of an alcoholic
and warps his mind. There was much talk about the mental state preceding the first drink.
pg157-p7-s1: "Yes, that's me," said the sick man, "the very image.
pg157-p7-s2:
You fellows know your stuff all right, but I don't see what good
it'll do.
pg157-p7-s4: I was once, but I'm a nobody now.
pg157-p7-s5:
From what you tell me, I know more than ever I
can't stop."
pg157-p7-s6: At this both the visitors burst into a laugh.
pg157-p7-s7:
Said the future Fellow Anonymous: "Damn little to laugh about
that I can see."
pg157-p7:
"Yes, that's me," said the sick man, "the very image. You fellows know your stuff all right, but I don't see what good
it'll do. You fellows are somebody. I was once, but I'm a nobody now. From what you tell me, I know more than ever I
can't stop." At this both the visitors burst into a laugh. Said the future Fellow Anonymous: "Damn little to laugh about
that I can see."
pg157-p8-s1: The two friends spoke of their spiritual experience and told him about the course of action they carried out.
pg157-p8: The two friends spoke of their spiritual experience and told him about the course of action they carried out.
